data.title: Citigroup must face $1B lawsuit claiming it aided Mexican oil company fraud - report
data.description:
Citigroup (NYSE:C) must face a reinstated lawsuit alleging it was responsible for over $1 billion in losses by facilitating and concealing a massive fraud at the now-bankrupt Mexican oil and gas services firm Oceanografía, according to a U.S. appeals court ruling reported by Reuters on Thursday.

A three-judge panel of the 11th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals in Miami said 30 Oceanografia vendors, creditors, and bondholders adequately alleged that Citigroup (NYSE:C) substantially aided the fraud, and a lower court judge erred in dismissing the nine-year-old case, the report said.

Citigroup's Banamex unit had provided cash advances to Oceanografia, which provided drilling services to Mexico's state-owned oil company Petroleos Mexicanos (Pemex), and collected interest payments on the advances.

According to the report, the plaintiffs, including shipping and leasing companies, investment funds and Netherlands-based Rabobank, said Citigroup (NYSE:C) advanced $3.3 billion to Oceanografia between 2008 and 2014 despite knowing the company had too much debt and had been forging Pemex signatures on authorization forms.

data.title: Nvidia to downgrade H20 chips for China after U.S. export restrictions - report
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) is set to release a downgraded version of its H20 artificial intelligence (AI) chip for the Chinese market within the next two months in response to tightened U.S. export restrictions that have blocked its sale, Reuters reported, citing sources familiar with the matter.

This move aims at maintaining the chipmaker's (NASDAQ:NVDA) presence in one of its biggest markets while complying with evolving U.S. regulations designed to limit China's access to advanced semiconductor technologies.

Nvidia (NDA) has worked on new technical specifications for the revised chip—including substantial reductions in performance, most notably a significant decrease in memory capacity and possibly other adjustments, according to the report.

China amounted to about 13% of Nvidia's total sales for the fiscal year ended January 2025. CEO Jensen Huang also visited Beijing last month to reinforce business relationships and stressed the importance of the Chinese market to the company.

Nvidia (NVDA) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

The H20 chip is central to China's AI development efforts, with customers ranging from startups like DeepSeek to tech giants such as Baidu and Alibaba.

It is likely that the downgrade is expected to make the H20 less competitive in China, potentially strengthening domestic rivals like Huawei, which already produces competing AI chips.

The U.S. government’s export controls are part of a broader effort to maintain a technological edge over China, especially in AI and semiconductor sectors. The need for a license to export the original H20 chip is the latest in a series of tightening restrictions, with both the Biden and Trump administrations supporting stricter controls.

data.title: Collegium pharmaceutical targets $135M in Jornay PM revenue for 2025 amid sales force expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Collegium Pharmaceutical (COLL) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Vikram Karnani highlighted the company’s commitment to three strategic priorities: driving growth in Jornay PM, maximizing the value of the pain portfolio, and strategic capital deployment. He reported 23% year-over-year revenue growth for Q1 2025, driven by Jornay PM’s performance.
  • Karnani confirmed the successful expansion of the Jornay PM sales force by 55 representatives, bringing the team to 180. This expansion targets an increase in prescriber engagement, with the aim of strengthening market penetration and prescription growth.
  • The company announced a $25 million accelerated share repurchase program to enhance shareholder value.
  • CFO Colleen Tupper reported net product revenues of $177.8 million for Q1 2025 and reiterated the company’s financial guidance for the year, including expected net product revenues of $735 million to $750 million and adjusted EBITDA of $435 million to $450 million.
  • Chief Commercial Officer Scott Dreyer emphasized Jornay PM’s unique evening dosing feature as a key differentiator in the ADHD market, driving its market share to 20.3% in the long-acting branded methylphenidate segment.

Outlook

  • The company reaffirmed its guidance for 2025, projecting Jornay PM net revenue to exceed $135 million, reflecting at least 34% annual growth.
  • Management expects the expanded sales force to significantly impact prescription growth beginning in late 2025, with full momentum anticipated in 2026 and beyond.
  • The durability of the pain portfolio was emphasized, with Xtampza ER, Belbuca, and Nucynta projected to continue contributing stable revenues through their extended exclusivity periods.

Financial Results

  • Jornay PM generated $28.5 million in net revenue in Q1 2025, representing a 24% year-over-year growth in prescriptions.
  • The pain portfolio delivered $149.2 million in revenue, a 3% year-over-year increase, with each pain medicine showing single-digit revenue growth.
  • Adjusted operating expenses for Q1 were $62.2 million, reflecting targeted investments in Jornay PM and sales force expansion.
  • Non-GAAP adjusted earnings per share for Q1 2025 were reported as $1.49.

Q&A

  • Les Sulewski, Truist Securities, inquired about the seasonal trends and sales force impacts on Jornay PM. Scott Dreyer responded that script growth typically slows during summer but accelerates in the back-to-school season, with tangible effects from the sales force expansion expected in Q4 2025 and into 2026.
  • Sulewski also asked about potential applications of Jornay’s delayed-release technology to other compounds. CEO Karnani confirmed prior discussions at Ironshore on this topic but noted no current plans for expansion beyond methylphenidate.
  • David Amsellem, Piper Sandler, queried about the sales force’s coverage and potential future expansion. Dreyer stated that the current team covers 60% of the long-acting ADHD market and that further expansions would be evaluated annually based on market needs.
  • Amsellem also asked about peak sales expectations for Jornay PM. Karnani indicated that specific projections would be better addressed after observing the results of the sales force expansion.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive sentiments about Jornay PM’s growth trajectory and the potential of the sales force expansion. Questions reflected interest in leveraging Jornay’s unique profile and its market position.
  • Management displayed confidence in Jornay PM’s prospects and the durability of its pain portfolio. Statements like "we are confident" and "we remain focused" highlighted their assertive outlook.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was consistent, emphasizing strategic growth and disciplined capital deployment.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Jornay PM at over $135 million in revenue remained unchanged from the previous quarter.
  • The expansion of the sales force from 125 to 180 representatives was completed, marking an operational milestone compared to Q4 2024.
  • The pain portfolio's performance showed consistent growth, with no significant changes in market trends or revenue expectations.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management addressed the risk of seasonal fluctuations in ADHD prescriptions, particularly during the summer months.
  • No material impact is expected from recent tariff policies, as Collegium’s medicines are primarily sourced and sold within the U.S.
  • Analysts raised concerns regarding the long-term durability of the pain portfolio amid generic competition post-2027.

Final Takeaway

Collegium Pharmaceutical’s Q1 2025 results emphasized strong growth in Jornay PM, supported by strategic investments in sales force expansion and marketing initiatives. The company reaffirmed its commitment to delivering over $135 million in Jornay PM revenue for 2025 and maintaining stable cash flow from its pain portfolio. With targeted investments and disciplined capital deployment, Collegium aims to strengthen its growth trajectory and shareholder value.
